suite.name: PAT

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

kw.name: Get Environment Variable
kw.doc: Returns the value of an environment variable with the given name.

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

kw.name: Run
kw.doc: Runs the given command in the system and returns the output.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Return From Keyword If
kw.doc: Returns from the enclosing user keyword if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: File Should Exist
kw.doc: Fails unless the given ``path`` points to an existing file.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

kw.name: Start Process
kw.doc: Starts a new process on background.

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

kw.name: Start Process
kw.doc: Starts a new process on background.

kw.name: Process Should Be Running
kw.doc: Verifies that the process is running.

kw.name: Run And Return Rc And Output
kw.doc: Runs the given command in the system and returns the RC and output.
kw.msg:
${output} = Client: Version: 26.0.0 Context: default Debug Mode: false
Server: ERROR: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running? errors pretty prin...

kw.name: Return From Keyword If
kw.doc: Returns from the enclosing user keyword if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Log
kw.arg: Docker daemon is not ready yet: ${output}
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.
kw.msg:
Docker daemon is not ready yet: Client: Version: 26.0.0 Context: default Debug Mode: false
Server: ERROR: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running? errors pretty printing info

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Run And Return Rc And Output
kw.doc: Runs the given command in the system and returns the RC and output.
kw.msg:
${output} = Client: Version: 26.0.0 Context: default Debug Mode: false
Server: ERROR: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running? errors pretty prin...

kw.doc: Returns from the enclosing user keyword if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Run And Return Rc And Output
kw.doc: Runs the given command in the system and returns the RC and output.

kw.name: Log
kw.arg: Docker daemon is not ready yet: ${output}
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.
kw.msg:
Docker daemon is not ready yet: Client: Version: 26.0.0 Context: default Debug Mode: false
Server: ERROR: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running? errors pretty printing info

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.arg: Docker daemon failed to become ready: ${output}
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

kw.name: Fail
kw.arg: Docker daemon did not become ready
kw.doc: Fails the test with the given message and optionally alters its tags.
kw.msg: Docker daemon did not become ready

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Run And Return Rc And Output
kw.doc: Runs the given command in the system and returns the RC and output.

kw.name: Should Be Equal As Integers
kw.doc: Fails if objects are unequal after converting them to integers.

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

kw.name: Compile and Up Harbor With Source Code
Run And Return Rc And Output
kw.doc: Runs the given command in the system and returns the RC and output.

kw.name: Should Be Equal As Integers
kw.doc: Fails if objects are unequal after converting them to integers.

kw.name: Log To Console
kw.doc: Logs the given message to the console.

test.name: Test Case - Admin Create PAT With Expiry
test.doc: Test creating a PAT with expiration date as admin
test.status: Parent suite setup failed: Docker daemon did not become ready

test.name: Test Case - PAT List Shows Creation And Expiration Dates
test.doc: Verify that creation and expiration dates display correctly in PAT list
test.status: Parent suite setup failed: Docker daemon did not become ready

test.name: Test Case - Refresh PAT Secret
test.doc: Test refreshing a PAT secret displays new secret in modal
test.status: Parent suite setup failed: Docker daemon did not become ready

test.name: Test Case - PAT Enable And Disable
test.doc: Test enabling and disabling a PAT
test.status: Parent suite setup failed: Docker daemon did not become ready

test.name: Test Case - Delete PAT
test.doc: Test deleting a PAT requires confirmation
test.status: Parent suite setup failed: Docker daemon did not become ready

test.name: Test Case - Non-Admin User Can Create And Manage Own PAT
test.doc: Test that non-admin users can create and manage their own PATs
test.status: Parent suite setup failed: Docker daemon did not become ready

test.name: Test Case - PAT Never Expires
test.doc: Test creating a PAT that never expires (0 days)
test.status: Parent suite setup failed: Docker daemon did not become ready

kw.name: Run And Return Rc And Output
kw.doc: Runs the given command in the system and returns the RC and output.
kw.msg:
${output} = fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/drone' To add an exception for this directory, call:
git config --global --add safe.directory /drone /bin/bash: go: command not found /bin/bash: ...

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.
kw.msg:
fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/drone' To add an exception for this directory, call:
git config --global --add safe.directory /drone /bin/bash: go: command not found /bin/bash: go: command not found stoping harbor instance... unknown shorthand flag: 'f' in -f See 'docker --help'.
Usage: docker [OPTIONS] COMMAND
A self-sufficient runtime for containers
Common Commands: run Create and run a new container from an image exec Execute a command in a running container ps List containers build Build an image from a Dockerfile pull Download an image from a registry push Upload an image to a registry images List images login Log in to a registry logout Log out from a registry search Search Docker Hub for images version Show the Docker version information info Display system-wide information
Management Commands: builder Manage builds checkpoint Manage checkpoints container Manage containers context Manage contexts image Manage images manifest Manage Docker image manifests and manifest lists network Manage networks plugin Manage plugins system Manage Docker trust Manage trust on Docker images volume Manage volumes
Swarm Commands: config Manage Swarm configs node Manage Swarm nodes secret Manage Swarm secrets service Manage Swarm services stack Manage Swarm stacks swarm Manage Swarm
Commands: attach Attach local standard input, output, and error streams to a running container commit Create a new image from a container's changes cp Copy files/folders between a container and the local filesystem create Create a new container diff Inspect changes to files or directories on a container's filesystem events Get real time events from the server export Export a container's filesystem as a tar archive history Show the history of an image import Import the contents from a tarball to create a filesystem image inspect Return low-level information on Docker objects kill Kill one or more running containers load Load an image from a tar archive or STDIN logs Fetch the logs of a container pause Pause all processes within one or more containers port List port mappings or a specific mapping for the container rename Rename a container restart Restart one or more containers rm Remove one or more containers rmi Remove one or more images save Save one or more images to a tar archive (streamed to STDOUT by default) start Start one or more stopped containers stats Display a live stream of container(s) resource usage statistics stop Stop one or more running containers tag Create a tag TARGET_IMAGE that refers to SOURCE_IMAGE top Display the running processes of a container unpause Unpause all processes within one or more containers update Update configuration of one or more containers wait Block until one or more containers stop, then print their exit codes
Global Options: --config string Location of client config files (default "/root/.docker") -c, --context string Name of the context to use to connect to the daemon (overrides DOCKER_HOST env var and default context set with "docker context use") -D, --debug Enable debug mode -H, --host list Daemon socket to connect to -l, --log-level string Set the logging level ("debug", "info", "warn", "error", "fatal") (default "info") --tls Use TLS; implied by --tlsverify --tlscacert string Trust certs signed only by this CA (default "/root/.docker/ca.pem") --tlscert string Path to TLS certificate file (default "/root/.docker/cert.pem") --tlskey string Path to TLS key file (default "/root/.docker/key.pem") --tlsverify Use TLS and verify the remote -v, --version Print version information and quit
Run 'docker COMMAND --help' for more information on a command.
For more help on how to use Docker, head to https://docs.docker.com/go/guides/
make: *** [Makefile:589: down] Error 125

kw.name: Should Be Equal As Integers
kw.doc: Fails if objects are unequal after converting them to integers.

suite.status:
Suite setup failed: Docker daemon did not become ready
Also suite teardown failed: 2 != 0
